title: Form 1099-MISC: Miscellaneous Information
content: Form 1099-MISC is used to report miscellaneous income that does not fall under other 1099 categories. Common uses include reporting rents, royalties, prizes and awards, medical and health care payments, and crop insurance proceeds. Prior to 2020, it was also used to report non-employee compensation (now reported on 1099-NEC). The payer files the form with the IRS and provides a copy to the recipient. Recipients must report this income on their tax return even if they do not receive the form, as long as the income exceeds the filing threshold.
